page: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized server configuration featuring 60 vCPUs and 240 GB of RAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon processor running at a clock speed of 3.1 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, utilizing 30 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled to deliver two threads per core. The processor includes 32 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 24 MB L3 cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, AES cryptography, and BMI2. This instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 61473.27 and a PassMark CPU score of 47758.67, both placing in the top 25% of tested servers. Memory bandwidth is also a key strength, with cached small-block reads reaching 3416.59 GB/sec and uncached large-block reads at 226.55 GB/sec. Conversely, single-core metrics, such as the Geekbench single-core score of 1317.0 and stress-ng single-core score of 1559.59, fall into the average tier.

While the instance demonstrates strong capabilities in database operations and multi-threaded decompression, it exhibits limitations in specific workloads. For example, LLM text generation for a small 135M model is weak at 60.5 tokens/sec, and there is no data for 70B model text generation. The lack of local storage and GPU accelerators makes it less suitable for direct hardware-accelerated deep learning training. The instance is best suited for compute-heavy workloads, multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving.
description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance configured with 60 vCPUs and 240 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 3.1 GHz, it features 30 physical cores with hyper-threading and dedicated CPU allocation. The hardware supports AVX-512 instructions but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth, placing in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Conversely, single-core performance and LLM text generation speeds are average to weak. This resource profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for high-density compute tasks that do not require local NVMe storage. It is suited for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and static web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 is a compute-optimized instance with 60 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 3.1 GHz CPU, delivering strong multi-core performance for database and compute-heavy workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c2-standard-60 features 60 vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, optimized for compute-heavy and database workloads.
tagline: A compute-optimized x86_64 instance featuring sixty dedicated vCPUs, high memory bandwidth, and strong multi-core performance for demanding parallel workloads.
bullet_points: 60 dedicated vCPUs and 240 GB RAM powered by a 3.1 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Strong multi-core CPU performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Supports AVX-512 vector and deep learning control instructions
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the configuration
Suited for database operations, multi-threaded applications, and web serving
